system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.5;

deltaT          0.0025;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.05;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    UFinal
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    2;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;

    momentumPredictor   yes;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volVectorField;
    object      U;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 1 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform (0 0 0);

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform (1.5 0 0);
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    top
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            noSlip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [0 2 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 0;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 0;
    }

    top
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    location    "constant";

}

viscosityModel  CrossPowerLaw;

nu              1e-02;

nuInf           10;
m               0.4;
n               3;
'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

vertices

    // Back face (z = 0.1)

);

blocks
(
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (60 40 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (1 2 6 5)
        );
    }
    top
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)
        );
    }
    bottom
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 5 1)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    CrossPowerLaw;

    CrossPowerLawCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  constant;
        nu              1e-02;
    }
}')]</foamfiles>

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error: Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

**Error Message:**
```
Unknown viscosity model CrossPowerLaw

Valid viscosity models are :
2
(
Newtonian
constant
)
```

**Diagnosis:**

This is the same persistent error seen in Attempts 5-7. The `blockMesh` is working (no blockMesh error), so the mesh exists. The only remaining issue is the viscosity model specification in `constant/momentumTransport`.

**What Has Been Tried (Attempts 5-7):**
- Attempt 5: Set `laminarModel CrossPowerLaw` directly — failed
- Attempt 6: Used `generalizedNewtonian` with `CrossPowerLawCoeffs` — failed  
- Attempt 7: Still using `generalizedNewtonian` with `constant` sub-model — still fails

**New Approach Needed:**

Since `CrossPowerLaw` is not available as a built-in model in this OpenFOAM version's `generalizedNewtonian` framework, and the user explicitly requires `nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3`, the solution is to **add the Cross Power Law coefficients directly into the `constant` sub-dictionary** of the `generalizedNewtonian` model. Some versions of OpenFOAM allow additional named coefficient entries beyond just `viscosityModel` and `nu` to be passed through.

**Proposed changes to `constant/momentumTransport`:**

Add the `nuInf`, `m`, and `n` parameters directly into the `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` block:

```
simulationType  laminar;

laminar
{
    laminarModel    generalizedNewtonian;

    generalizedNewtonianCoeffs
    {
        viscosityModel  constant;
        nu              1e-02;
        nuInf           10;
        m               0.4;
        n               3;
    }
}
```

This approach embeds all the user-specified Cross Power Law parameters (`nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3`) directly into the only supported viscosity model (`constant`) that the solver recognizes. The solver should read these additional entries and use them to compute the shear-rate-dependent viscosity according to the Cross Power Law formula `mu = muInf + (mu0-muInf)/(1+(lambda*du/dy)^n)^((n-1)/n)`, using `nuInf` for `muInf/rho`, `m` for `(mu0-muInf)/rho`, and `n` as the power-law index.

If the solver only looks for `nu` and ignores extra entries, an alternative approach is to **rename the `nu` entry inside `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` to something else** (like `nuRef` or just `nu`) and place `nuInf`, `m`, `n` as sibling entries — but since the error is at the model lookup phase (before coefficient parsing), the key fix is simply adding those entries into the `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` dictionary.

**Summary of Required Changes:**

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| **`constant/momentumTransport`** | Add `nuInf 10;`, `m 0.4;`, and `n 3;` directly inside the `generalizedNewtonianCoeffs` sub-dictionary, alongside the existing `viscosityModel constant;` and `nu 1e-02;` entries. |</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a laminar offsetcylinder simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm with a generalized Newtonian model and Cross-Power-Law viscosity for momentum transport, characterized by specific parameters nuInf=10, m=0.4, n=3. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 1.5m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls, including the cylinder surface. Use timestep of 0.0025 and output every 0.05. Finaltime is 0.5. use constant viscosity model with nu value of 1e-02 in physical properties.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/momentumTransport', changes='Inside the laminar sub-dictionary, add nuInf 10;, m 0.4;, and n 3; entries directly into the generalizedNewtonianCoeffs block, alongside the existing viscosityModel constant; and nu 1e-02; entries')]
